approach_rationale: Smallest patch (1-2 lines) inside ShoppingListService.can_merge (mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py:45-71) and/or ShoppingListService.merge_items (:73-128). Strict match for input intent which forbids surrounding refactors and mandates minimum scope. Lowest blast radius and preserves all pre-existing green tests in test_group_shopping_lists.py, test_group_shopping_list_items.py, and test_group_mealplan.py. See approach/selected.md.
problem_statement: When a user schedules the same recipe in multiple meal-plan slots and triggers 'Add Meal Plan to Shopping List', the resulting shopping list must contain exactly one row per (food_id, unit_id) whose quantity equals the recipe's per-ingredient quantity times the number of occurrences. Today this invariant is violated either by lost accumulation (single-recipe quantity shown) or lost consolidation (multiple unmerged rows). Defect lives in ShoppingListService.can_merge or ShoppingListService.merge_items in mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py.

user_stories.title: 复现 (Reproduce)
user_stories.i_want: an integration test that reliably reproduces the meal-plan-to-shopping-list consolidation failure
user_stories.so_that: the regression cannot recur silently and the fix has a measurable acceptance signal
acceptance_criteria: A new test file tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_meal_plan_to_shopping_bug.py exists.

The test test_meal_plan_to_shopping_list_accumulates_duplicate_recipe_ingredients creates food_tomato and food_egg via unique_user.repos.ingredient_foods.create(SaveIngredientFood(name=..., group_id=unique_user.group_id)).
It creates two units via unique_user.repos.ingredient_units.create(SaveIngredientUnit(name='each' / 'tsp', group_id=unique_user.group_id)) with standard_unit=None (default).

It creates recipe_a via unique_user.repos.recipes.create(Recipe(... recipe_ingredient=[RecipeIngredient(food=food_tomato, unit=unit_each, quantity=2.0, note=food_tomato.name), RecipeIngredient(food=food_salt, unit=unit_tsp, quantity=1.0, note=food_salt.name)])).

It creates two meal-plan entries via POST /api/households/mealplans using CreatePlanEntry(date=monday, entry_type='dinner', recipe_id=recipe_a.id).model_dump(by_alias=True) with date string and recipeId string normalization (per tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_group_mealplan.py:80-99).
It acquires the destination shopping list from the shopping_list fixture (tests/fixtures/fixture_shopping_lists.py:49-65).

It POSTs [ShoppingListAddRecipeParamsBulk(recipe_id=recipe_a.id), ShoppingListAddRecipeParamsBulk(recipe_id=recipe_a.id)] (two per-occurrence entries) to api_routes.households_shopping_lists_item_id_recipe(shopping_list.id) using utils.jsonify([... .model_dump()]).

GETs the shopping list and asserts exactly one item per food: tomato quantity == 4.0 (within 1e-6) with unit_id == unit_each.id and food_id == food_tomato.id; salt quantity == 2.0 with unit_id == unit_tsp.id and food_id == food_salt.id; total len(listItems) == 2.
This test FAILS on the buggy revision and PASSES after the US-3 fix.

user_stories.title: 根因 (Root-cause analysis)
user_stories.i_want: the PR description to contain a structured root-cause analysis
user_stories.so_that: the failure mode, fix location, and adjacent contract are documented for future maintainers
acceptance_criteria: PR description contains a '### Root cause' section answering, in order: (1) which function holds the bug with file path + symbol + exact line number(s); (2) whether the variant is overwrite-vs-accumulate (input.md附录 Variant A, merge_items line 96) or wrong merge key (Variant B, can_merge line 71); (3) boundary cases enumerated explicitly.

Boundary-case enumeration covers: same food_id + same unit_id ⇒ merge with sum; same food_id + different unit_id with both standard_unit=None ⇒ no merge; different food_id even with same display ⇒ no merge; food_id is None ⇒ merge falls back to note equality (can_merge line 71); recipe_scale accumulation through merge_items lines 109-126 yields one recipe_references entry with recipe_scale == N when same recipe_id is added N times.
Each answer cites at least one specific file:line reference inside mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py.

user_stories.title: 最小修复 (Minimum-scope fix)
user_stories.i_want: a 1-2 line surgical patch inside mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py that restores the canonical (food_id, unit_id) merge key and the to_item.quantity += from_item.quantity accumulation
user_stories.so_that: the reproduction passes and no other behavior is affected
acceptance_criteria: Only mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py is modified. No other production file changes.
Diff is confined to ShoppingListService.can_merge (lines 45-71) and/or ShoppingListService.merge_items (lines 73-128).
No new methods, no extracted helpers, no signature changes, no schema changes.
The non-unit-converted branch at line 96 contains to_item.quantity += from_item.quantity (NOT =).
can_merge rejects items with item1.food_id != item2.food_id (line 52) and uses bool(item1.food_id) or item1.note == item2.note as final return (line 71); display is NOT used as a merge key anywhere.
bulk_create_items (lines 154-223), bulk_update_items (lines 225-310), get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe (lines 323-411), and add_recipe_ingredients_to_list (lines 413-455) are unchanged.

All pre-existing tests in tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_group_shopping_lists.py and tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_group_shopping_list_items.py continue to pass with no modifications.

user_stories.title: 回归测试 (Regression tests)
user_stories.i_want: four additional regression tests in the same new test file that pin the exact merge contract
user_stories.so_that: any future change to consolidation logic is caught immediately
acceptance_criteria: test_single_occurrence: schedule the recipe in exactly one meal-plan slot, add to fresh shopping list. Assert each ingredient appears once with quantity == ingredient.quantity (within 1e-6), food_id and unit_id set, exactly one recipe_references entry with recipe_scale == 1.0; len(listItems) == 2.

test_multiple_occurrences_same_unit: parametrized via @pytest.mark.parametrize('occurrences', [2, 3]). Schedule the recipe N times in distinct meal-plan slots, add via N per-occurrence bulk entries. Assert each ingredient appears once with quantity == ingredient.quantity * N, exactly one recipe_references entry with recipe_scale == float(N); len(listItems) == 2.

test_multiple_occurrences_different_units: build TWO recipes — recipe_each with RecipeIngredient(food=food_tomato, unit=unit_each, quantity=2) and recipe_grams with RecipeIngredient(food=food_tomato, unit=unit_gram, quantity=100); both units created with standard_unit=None. Schedule one of each, add both. Assert TWO distinct list items for food_tomato, one with unit_id == unit_each.id quantity == 2.0, one with unit_id == unit_gram.id quantity == 100.0; same food_id, distinct unit_id; len(listItems) == 2.

test_different_food_same_name: create two SaveIngredientFood(name='tomato', group_id=...) records (distinct UUIDs, identical name). Build two recipes each using a different food but same unit_each and quantity=2. Schedule one of each, add both. Assert TWO distinct list items, each with food.name == 'tomato' but DISTINCT food_id, each with quantity == 2.0; len(listItems) == 2. Confirms food_id (not display/name) is the merge key.
All four tests PASS after the US-3 fix.

functional_requirements.description: A new test file tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_meal_plan_to_shopping_bug.py contains test_meal_plan_to_shopping_list_accumulates_duplicate_recipe_ingredients. Runnable via 'task py:test -- tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_meal_plan_to_shopping_bug.py' or 'uv run pytest <path>'. Exercises the real HTTP route POST /api/households/shopping/lists/{item_id}/recipe and asserts per-food quantity and row-count invariants on the response of the subsequent GET /api/households/shopping/lists/{item_id}.

PR description contains a '### Root cause' section answering (1) which function holds the bug with path:line, (2) which variant (A=overwrite-vs-accumulate at merge_items line 96, or B=wrong merge key at can_merge line 71), and (3) boundary cases. Variant labeling references input.md附录 patches. Boundary cases cover: same food+unit merges with sum; same food + different unit (standard_unit=None) does not merge; different food_id with same display does not merge; food_id is None falls back to note equality; recipe_scale accumulation produces a single ref with recipe_scale == N.

Diff modifies only mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py. Within that file, only ShoppingListService.can_merge (lines 45-71) and/or ShoppingListService.merge_items (lines 73-128) are touched. Total changed lines <= 5. After the fix: can_merge keeps 'item1.food_id != item2.food_id' rejection at line 52, retains the standard_unit / UnitConverter branch (lines 57-68), and returns 'bool(item1.food_id) or item1.note == item2.note' at line 71. merge_items keeps 'to_item.quantity += from_item.quantity' at line 96, merge_quantity_and_unit branch (lines 86-92), note concatenation, extras update, and recipe-reference merge (lines 109-126) unchanged.

code_references.purpose: can_merge — predicate to repair if Variant B
merge_items — accumulator to repair if Variant A

functional_requirements.description: Four regression tests are appended to test_meal_plan_to_shopping_bug.py with exact names and behaviors: test_single_occurrence, test_multiple_occurrences_same_unit (parametrized over [2, 3]), test_multiple_occurrences_different_units, test_different_food_same_name. Each asserts both per-item quantity AND len(listItems) count; each asserts food_id and unit_id on returned items (not just note/display) to lock the merge-key semantics.

code_references.purpose: ShoppingListItemOut exposes food_id, unit_id, food, unit
Analogous prior test pattern to mirror (test_shopping_lists_add_recipes_with_merge)

functional_requirements.description: After the US-3 fix, all tests in test_group_shopping_lists.py, test_group_shopping_list_items.py, and test_group_mealplan.py PASS without modification. In particular, the existing merge tests test_shopping_lists_add_recipe_with_merge (lines 581-660), test_shopping_lists_add_recipes_with_merge (lines 663-739), and test_shopping_lists_add_nested_recipe_ingredients (lines 249-361) keep passing — confirming no regression of PR #5054 (bulk add), #4800 (recipe-as-ingredient), or #7121 (unit standardization).

code_references.purpose: Standard-unit merge tests must remain green

functional_requirements.description: Tests follow Mealie conventions: use unique_user + api_client fixtures; assert via utils.assert_deserialize(response, 200) (tests/utils/assertion_helpers.py:23-25); serialize bulk payloads via utils.jsonify([... .model_dump()]) (tests/utils/jsonify.py:1-5); deserialize list response via ShoppingListOut.model_validate(...) for typed access to food, unit, food_id, unit_id, recipe_references. Full validation is 'task py:check' (Taskfile.yml:122-128). Python entrypoints use uv (never python/pip).

success_criteria.measurement: uv run pytest tests/integration_tests/user_household_tests/test_meal_plan_to_shopping_bug.py::test_meal_plan_to_shopping_list_accumulates_duplicate_recipe_ingredients on the buggy HEAD

success_criteria.metric: Same test exit status after fix

success_criteria.threshold: 5/5 named tests PASS (parametrized expansion may yield more)
0 new failures across test_group_shopping_lists.py, test_group_shopping_list_items.py, test_group_mealplan.py

edge_cases.scenario: Same food_id, different unit_id, neither unit has standard_unit
edge_cases.source: mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py:57-68 — can_merge fails standard_unit check on both items and returns False.

edge_cases.scenario: Different food_id, identical food.name ('tomato' twice with distinct UUIDs)
edge_cases.source: mealie/services/household_services/shopping_lists.py:52 — can_merge rejects when item1.food_id != item2.food_id regardless of name/display.

edge_cases.scenario: Same recipe added N times via mixed recipe_increment_quantity (e.g. one bulk entry with 2 and one with 1)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: One row per (food_id, unit_id) with quantity = base * 3; one recipe_references entry with recipe_scale == 3.0.
Assertions use pytest.approx(..., abs=1e-6) or pytest.approx(..., rel=1e-6); quantities are persisted as raw float sums (no rounding after merge).
edge_cases.source: mealie/db/models/household/shopping_list.py:67 (Float column); mealie/schema/recipe/recipe_ingredient.py:23 and 345-357 round INPUT to INGREDIENT_QTY_PRECISION=3 but merge_items line 96 does NOT round the SUM.

edge_cases.scenario: Recipe deleted between meal-plan creation and add-to-shopping-list
edge_cases.expected_behavior: get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe raises UnexpectedNone('Recipe not found'); the new tests do NOT exercise this path (out of fix scope), but the spec documents it as an unchanged contract.

edge_cases.scenario: food_id is None on both items (parsed/unparsed ingredient with no food)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Merges only when note matches (can_merge line 71 fallback: return bool(item1.food_id) or item1.note == item2.note).

edge_cases.scenario: Recipe with internal duplicate ingredients AND recipe_increment_quantity > 1 (sub-recipe scaling)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: The same-recipe pre-merge at lines 395-397 adds raw ingredient.quantity (not scaled); this is a separate latent issue documented in self_concerns, NOT fixed here.

self_concerns.concern: In-recipe duplicate scaling latent bug at get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe lines 395-397: existing_item.quantity += ingredient.quantity instead of ingredient.quantity * scale. With recipe_increment_quantity > 1 and internal duplicates, undercounts. OUT OF SCOPE for case 3 (different bug surface). Recommended follow-up: separate PR with its own reproduction test.

Float-precision accumulation not rounded after merge. merge_items line 96 produces raw float sums; INGREDIENT_QTY_PRECISION=3 only applies to input rounding, not consolidated sums. Tests in this spec use pytest.approx(..., abs=1e-6) to remain robust. A future enhancement could re-round at the persistence boundary — separate PR.

Future unit-conversion merge dimensions: PR #7121 made compatible standard_unit values merge. If a future PR adds more dimensions (e.g. fuzzy food matching by alias), test_multiple_occurrences_different_units must be re-evaluated. Spec uses standard_unit=None to insulate the regression.

out_of_scope: Refactoring bulk_create_items, bulk_update_items, get_shopping_list_items_from_recipe, or add_recipe_ingredients_to_list.
Any SQLAlchemy model, Alembic migration, repository, or Pydantic schema change.
Adding a dedicated meal-plan-to-shopping-list backend route.
Fixing in-recipe duplicate scaling latent bug (see SCN-1).
Re-rounding float sums after merge (see SCN-2).
OpenAPI generation, TypeScript codegen, or test-helper generation (task dev:generate NOT required since no Pydantic schemas change).
